what: The frozen rules, and where they come from
title: ABA as a methodological protocol for reinforcement learning
text: protocol fixes parameters , ABA literature, rather leaving per-analysis discretion. Steady-state reading. condition read responding settled. Following steady-state tradition (Sidman) concrete practice reported operant work (minimum number sessions per condition, mean last several stability criterion met; McSweeney & Murphy, 2014), stability_reached() checks last several observations show trend bounded bounce, reading happens non-stabilisation reported. decision rule. Subjective visual analysis low inter-rater reliability, protocol uses criterion-line rule (Hagopian et al., 1997; described Fisher et al., 2021): draw upper lower line one standard deviation control mean, call test condition differentiated number points upper line minus lower line least half data points. criterion_line_verdict() implements exactly , verdict degree freedom. Idiographic replication. function established per subject never pooling response rates across subjects, manufacture group function subject . functional_analysis_replicated() runs subjects reliability conclusion summarises per-subject verdicts read time.
code: flat <- tibble::tibble(session = 1:12, condition = "play",                        rate = c(rep(0.2, 6), 0.21, 0.19, 0.2, 0.2, 0.21, 0.2)) trending <- tibble::tibble(session = 1:12, condition = "play",                            rate = seq(0.1, 0.9, length.out = 12)) c(flat = stability_reached(flat), trending = stability_reached(trending)) #>     flat trending  #>     TRUE    FALSE ctrl <- c(0.04, 0.05, 0.06, 0.05, 0.05) last_k <- tibble::tibble(   session = rep(1:5, 5),   condition = rep(c("attention", "escape", "tangible", "goal", "play"), each = 5),   rate = c(rep(0.05, 5), rep(0.9, 5), rep(0.05, 5), rep(0.05, 5), ctrl) ) criterion_line_verdict(last_k)$verdict #> [1] "escape"

what: LunarLander: the protocol as a value-add on a real solve
title: ABA as a methodological protocol for reinforcement learning
text: logic applies real control task. Eight policies trained LunarLander-v3 evaluated across 200 terrains; returns bundled. Four DQN policies (seeds 0–3, near-solved worse); four PPO policies (seeds 99–102) trained common budget clean chunked resume, four solve (true means 243, 219, 238 242). Held disjoint terrain set, first PPO policy still scores 227, confirming solve sample. solved? short evaluation coin flip. Across terrain pools five episodes , roughly half declare seed 0 solved half ; protocol reads settled estimate reports solved, bootstrap confidence attached. policy best? two near-identical policies ad hoc head--head winner flips evaluation; protocol compares settled estimates difference band calls indistinguishable. precise sense best policy can evaluation-sample artifact. Training-seed reliability. fixed budget seeds split: reach high settled return, fail near zero. claim resting one trained seed reproducible. four PPO policies (seeds 99–102) clear criterion; four DQN policies . two algorithms differ kind lottery run: DQN’s catastrophic — two four seeds fail outright — PPO’s, larger budget, governs solve quality rather solve--fail (settled estimates spread 215 243). Even genuinely solved policy short evaluation occasionally reads solved, protocol confirms full bootstrap confidence; protocol thus correct directions — false positives near-threshold DQN policies, false negatives solved ones.
code: returns <- lunar_returns() round(tapply(returns$ret, returns$policy_seed, mean), 1) #>     0     1     2     3    99   100   101   102  #> 182.3 166.8  -1.2   6.4 242.9 218.5 237.7 242.3 d <- lunar_solved_convergence(policy_seed = 0L) c(adhoc_distinct_verdicts = d$adhoc_distinct,   protocol = d$protocol_verdict,   estimate = round(d$protocol_estimate, 1),   boot_P_solved = round(d$bootstrap_solved_fraction, 3)) #> adhoc_distinct_verdicts                protocol                estimate  #>                     "2"            "not solved"                 "173.6"  #>           boot_P_solved  #>                 "0.017" b <- lunar_best_policy_convergence(policy_a = 0L, policy_b = 1L) c(adhoc_distinct_winners = b$adhoc_distinct, protocol = b$protocol_verdict,   est0 = round(b$estimate_a, 1), est1 = round(b$estimate_b, 1)) #> adhoc_distinct_winners               protocol                   est0  #>                    "2"    "indistinguishable"                "173.6"  #>                   est1  #>                "172.9" as.data.frame(lunar_training_reliability()$per_seed) #>   policy_seed estimate stable    verdict #> 1           0    173.6   TRUE not solved #> 2           1    172.9   TRUE not solved #> 3           2     -0.7   TRUE not solved #> 4           3      0.1   TRUE not solved #> 5          99    243.5   TRUE     solved #> 6         100    214.6   TRUE     solved #> 7         101    235.9   TRUE     solved #> 8         102    235.0   TRUE     solved ds <- lunar_solved_convergence(policy_seed = 99L) c(protocol = ds$protocol_verdict, estimate = round(ds$protocol_estimate, 1),   boot_P_solved = round(ds$bootstrap_solved_fraction, 2), adhoc_distinct = ds$adhoc_distinct) #>       protocol       estimate  boot_P_solved adhoc_distinct  #>       "solved"        "243.5"            "1"            "2" plot_lunar_convergence(d)

title: Differentiating reward maximization from melioration
text: Two accounts describe choice tracks reinforcement. law effect says behavior moves toward action highest long-run return: reward maximization. Melioration (mechanism behind matching law) says behavior moves toward action higher local return, evaluated myopically. many environments two coincide. come apart choosing option changes local returns, smaller-sooner reward competes larger-later one. operantlunar holds learning skeleton fixed swaps update rule, runs rule operant paradigms designed separate two accounts.

title: operantlunar: Differentiating Reward Maximization from Melioration via Operant Conditioning — operantlunar-package
text: tabular reinforcement-learning instrument mechanistically differentiates reward-maximizing control (law effect) descriptive melioration (matching law). Provides shared learning skeleton; library learning rules (Q-learning, SARSA, expected SARSA, double Q-learning, actor-critic, model-based planner, gradient-bandit rate-tracking melioration, win-stay-lose-shift); battery operant paradigms (schedule family, melioration trap, extinction, probability matching, self-control / delay discounting, differential reinforcement low rates, progressive ratio, risk sensitivity, reinforcer devaluation, changeover-delay concurrent schedules); analysis layer generalized-matching, Herrnstein-hyperbola discounting fits differentiation-matrix capstone; hashed tile coding linear function-approximation agents generic Gymnasium adapter; optional Python LunarLander environment via reticulate; bundled Shiny app.
